questions.text: Which of the following gives the relationship between linear velocity and angular velocity?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The greater the rotation angle in a given amount of time, the greater the angular velocity. Angular velocity (ω) is analogous to linear velocity (v). We can write the relationship between linear velocity and angular velocity in two different ways:v=rw or ω=v/r.

It is incorrect as per the given explanation.v = rθ. This formula is incorrect. While θ represents angular displacement, it doesn't directly relate to linear velocity in the same way angular velocity (ω) does.

v = sω and v = sθ. These options are also incorrect. 's' typically represents arc length, and while related, these formulas don't accurately describe the fundamental relationship between linear and angular velocity.

questions.explanation: The greater the rotation angle in a given amount of time, the greater the angular velocity. Angular velocity (ω) is analogous to linear velocity (v). We can write the relationship between linear velocity and angular velocity in two different ways: v=rω or ω=v/r.

questions.text: The ratio of angular speed of moon around the Earth to its angular speed about its own axis is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is not correct as per calculations
This is not correct as per calculations
This is not correct as per calculations

The moon orbits the Earth once every 27.322 days. In the same amount of time, the moon rotates once on its axis. As a result, the moon does not seem to be spinning but appears to observers from Earth to be keeping almost perfectly still (this is called synchronous rotation). So its angular speed (W) around its own axis isW =2π /TW(moon) = (2π)/(27.322 x 24 x 3600) = 2.67*10(-6) rad/sFor orbital motion around the earth, the angular speed isW(earth) = (2π)/(27.322 x 24 x 3600) = 2.67*10(-6) rad/sSo the ratio is 1:1

questions.explanation: The moon orbits the Earth once every 27.322 days. In the same amount of time, the moon rotates once on its axis. As a result, the moon does not seem to be spinning but appears to observers from Earth to be keeping almost perfectly still (this is called synchronous rotation). So its angular speed (W) around its own axis isW =2π /TW(moon) = (2π)/(27.322 x 24 x 3600) = 2.67*10(-6) rad/sFor orbital motion around the earth, the angular speed isW(earth) = (2π)/(27.322 x 24 x 3600) = 2.67*10(-6) rad/sSo the ratio is 1:1

questions.text: Law of conservation of momentum states that:I. there is no external force applied to a system, then the total momentum of that system remains constant II. if there is an external force applied to a system, then the total momentum of that system remains constant III. if there is no external force applied to a system, then the total momentum of that system keeps changing
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Right answer. The law of conservation of momentum states that, for two or more bodies in an isolated system acting upon each other, their total momentum remains constant unless an external force is applied. Therefore, momentum can neither be created nor destroyed and will stay constant.

Wrong answer. Law of conservation of momentum states that. For two or more bodies in an isolated system acting upon each other, their total momentum remains constant unless an external force is applied. Therefore, momentum can neither be created nor destroyed.

Wrong answer. Law of conservation of momentum states that. For two or more bodies in an isolated system acting upon each other, their total momentum remains constant unless an external force is applied. Therefore, momentum can neither be created nor destroyed.

Wrong answer. Law of conservation of momentum states that. For two or more bodies in an isolated system acting upon each other, their total momentum remains constant unless an external force is applied. Therefore, momentum can neither be created nor destroyed.

Wrong answer. Law of conservation of momentum states that. For two or more bodies in an isolated system acting upon each other, their total momentum remains constant unless an external force is applied. Therefore, momentum can neither be created nor destroyed.

questions.explanation: If there is no external force applied to the system, then the total momentum of that system remains constant. If an external force is applied then the total momentum will change.The law of conservation of momentum states that, for two or more bodies in an isolated system acting upon each other, their total momentum remains constant unless an external force is applied. Therefore, momentum can neither be created nor destroyed and will stay constant.

options.explanation: Current is often represented by I in physics, but it is unrelated to the equation for angular momentum.
Length is typically denoted by L, not I, and does not pertain to this equation.
Angular momentum is represented by I in the context of this equation, which is the cross product of the position vector r and the linear momentum vector p.
Torque is usually denoted by the lowercase Greek letter tau (τ) and is not related to this specific equation.
Displacement is represented by the symbol d, and it is not relevant to this equation.

questions.explanation: In three dimensions, the angular momentum for a point article is a pseudovector r × p, the cross product of the particle's position vector r (relative to some origin), and its momentum vector.

questions.text: A rotating wheel of radius 0.5 m has an angular velocity of 5 rad/s at some instant and 10 rad / s after 5 s. Find the angular acceleration of a point on its rim.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A is correct as ωf=ωi+αt.α= (ωf- ωi)/t= (10-5)/5=1rad/s2.Hence all other options are rendered as incorrect.
It is incorrect as per the given formula.Option A is correct as ωf=ωi+αt.α= (ωf- ωi)/t= (10-5)/5=1rad/s2.Hence all other options are rendered as incorrect.
It is incorrect as per the given formula. Option A is correct as ωf=ωi+αt.α= (ωf- ωi)/t= (10-5)/5=1rad/s2.Hence all other options are rendered as incorrect.
It is incorrect as per the given formula. Option A is correct as ωf=ωi+αt.α= (ωf- ωi)/t= (10-5)/5=1rad/s2.Hence all other options are rendered as incorrect.
It is incorrect as per the given formula. Option A is correct as ωf=ωi+αt.α= (ωf- ωi)/t= (10-5)/5=1rad/s2.Hence all other options are rendered as incorrect.

questions.explanation: The angular acceleration of a rotating object can be found using the formula α = (ωf - ωi) / t, where ωf is the final angular velocity, ωi is the initial angular velocity, and t is the time interval. In this case, the initial angular velocity (ωi) is 5 rad/s, the final angular velocity (ωf) is 10 rad/s, and the time interval (t) is 5 seconds. Substituting these values into the formula gives: α = (10 rad/s - 5 rad/s) / 5 s = 1 rad/s2. Therefore, Option A is correct. All other options are incorrect as they do not result from applying this formula to the given values.

questions.text: If during circular motion, the tangential velocity of a body becomes double, then centripetal force becomes:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is not correct. If the tangential velocity of a body becomes double during circular motion, the centripetal force required does not double but increases by a greater factor.
This option is also not correct. Doubling the tangential velocity does not result in the centripetal force becoming one-half of its initial value; it increases.

When an object moves in a circular motion, it experiences a centripetal force that keeps it moving in a curved path. The centripetal force is given by the equation: F = (mv²) / r, where F is the centripetal force, m is the mass of the object, v is the tangential velocity, and r is the radius of the circular path. If the tangential velocity of the body becomes double, we can denote the original velocity as v and the new velocity as 2v. We can calculate the new centripetal force as F' = (m(2v)²) / r = (4mv²) / r. Comparing F' to the original force F, we can see that F' is indeed four times the value of F.
This option is not correct. When the tangential velocity becomes double, the centripetal force doesn't become one-fourth of its initial value. Instead, it increases, as indicated in option c.

questions.explanation: When an object moves in a circular motion, it experiences a centripetal force that keeps it moving in a curved path. The centripetal force is given by the equation: F = (mv²) / r, where F is the centripetal force, m is the mass of the object, v is the tangential velocity, and r is the radius of the circular path. If the tangential velocity of the body becomes double, we can denote the original velocity as v and the new velocity as 2v. We can calculate the new centripetal force as F' = (m(2v)²) / r = (4mv²) / r. Comparing F' to the original force F, we can see that F' is indeed four times the value of F.

questions.text: The rate of change of angular velocity with respect to _ defines angular acceleration.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Speed is a scalar quantity that refers to how fast an object is moving along a path. It does not involve direction and is unrelated to angular quantities like velocity or acceleration.
Frequency measures how often an event occurs over a period of time. While it is related to periodic processes, it is not directly involved in defining angular acceleration.
Distance is a scalar measure of how much ground an object has covered, regardless of its starting or ending position. It is not a factor in determining angular acceleration.

Time is the critical variable over which changes in angular velocity are measured to calculate angular acceleration. It is the correct answer as angular acceleration is defined as the change in angular velocity per unit of time, typically expressed in radians per second squared (rad/s^2).
Gravity is a force that affects the motion of objects, but it is not directly related to the concept of angular acceleration, which is a measure of how quickly the rotational speed is changing.

questions.explanation: Angular acceleration is defined as the rate of change of angular velocity with respect to time. This means it describes how quickly the speed of rotation is changing, and is typically measured in radians per second squared (rad/s^2). None of the other options—speed, frequency, distance, or gravity—relate directly to this rate of change. Speed and distance are linear measures, frequency is a measure of cycles per time but not change rate, and gravity is a force, not a measure of change in motion over time.

questions.text: A body having translatory motion possesses _ and _. In the same way a body having rotatory motion possesses _ and _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect as both translatory and rotatory motions have specific properties associated with them.

options.text: Linear velocity... Linear momentum... Angular velocity... Angular momentum
options.explanation: Correct. Translatory motion involves linear properties - linear velocity and momentum, while rotatory motion involves angular properties - angular velocity and momentum.

options.text: Linear momentum... Angular momentum... Linear velocity... Angular velocity
options.explanation: Incorrect. The properties are mismatched with the types of motion. Translatory motion should be associated with linear properties, and rotatory with angular properties.

options.text: Angular velocity... Angular momentum... Linear momentum... Linear velocity
options.explanation: Incorrect. Angular and linear properties are swapped for the types of motion they should be associated with.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Linear velocity... Linear momentum... Angular velocity... Angular momentum.In translatory motion, all points of a body move uniformly in a single direction, involving linear properties such as linear velocity and linear momentum. This type of motion does not change the orientation of the object.In contrast, rotatory motion occurs when a body rotates around its own axis, involving angular properties like angular velocity and angular momentum. For example, the Earth's rotation about its axis is a type of rotatory motion.All options except B do not properly align the motion types with their respective properties, which is why they are incorrect.

questions.text: The physical quantity which produces angular acceleration in the body is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Force is responsible for translational motion of a body.
Moment of inertia is the rotational analogue of mass. It plays the same role as mass in translational motion.
Impulse is the rate of change of momentum of a body.
Torque is the rotational analog of force. It plays the same role in rotational motion as force in translational motion. Torque causes rotational acceleration.

questions.explanation: Torque is a measure of the force that can cause an object to rotate about an axis. Just as force is what causes an object to accelerate in linear kinematics, torque is what causes an object to acquire angular acceleration

questions.text: When brakes of a car are applied, angular velocity of a flywheel reduces from 900 cycle / min to 720 cycle / min in 6 sec. Angular retardation is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: First, we need to convert the given angular velocities from cycles per minute to radians per second. We know that 1 cycle is equal to 2π radians. Therefore,Angular velocity at the start = 900 cycles/min = (900 x 2π) / 60 rad/s = 30π rad/sAngular velocity at the end = 720 cycles/min = (720 x 2π) / 60 rad/s = 24π rad/sThe time taken for the flywheel to reduce its angular velocity from 30π rad/s to 24π rad/s is 6 seconds. We can use the formula for angular retardation:Angular retardation (α) = (ω2 - ω1) / tWhere ω2 is the final angular velocity, ω1 is the initial angular velocity, and t is the time taken.Substituting the given values, we get:Angular retardation (α) = (24π - 30π) / 6 = -π rad/s^2Therefore, the angular retardation is -π rad/s^2.
Incorrect according to the given solution.
Incorrect according to the solution.
Incorrect according to the solution.
Incorrect. The data given is sufficient.

questions.explanation: This is the following solution.First, we need to convert the given angular velocities from cycles per minute to radians per second. We know that 1 cycle is equal to 2π radians. Therefore,Angular velocity at the start = 900 cycles/min = (900 x 2π) / 60 rad/s = 30π rad/sAngular velocity at the end = 720 cycles/min = (720 x 2π) / 60 rad/s = 24π rad/sThe time taken for the flywheel to reduce its angular velocity from 30π rad/s to 24π rad/s is 6 seconds. We can use the formula for angular retardation:Angular retardation (α) = (ω2 - ω1) / tWhere ω2 is the final angular velocity, ω1 is the initial angular velocity, and t is the time taken.Substituting the given values, we get:Angular retardation (α) = (24π - 30π) / 6 = -π rad/s^2Therefore, the angular retardation is -π rad/s^2.

questions.text: A body moves on a circular path subtending an angle of 2 radians at the center of a circle of radius 3.5 meters. The linear displacement of the body is _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it does not accurately apply the formula for linear displacement.
This is correct answer
This option is incorrect because it overestimates the linear displacement based on the given parameters.
This option is incorrect as it significantly underestimates the linear displacement derived from the radius and angle.

questions.explanation: The linear displacement of a body moving along a circular path can be determined using the formula:Linear Displacement = 2 × Radius × sin(θ/2)Here, the radius (r) is 3.5 meters, and the angle (θ) is 2 radians. Plugging in the values, we apply:Linear Displacement = 2 × 3.5 × sin(2/2) = 2 × 3.5 × sin(1) ≈ 7 metersTherefore, the correct linear displacement of the body is 7 meters. All other options (6.5 m, 5.8 m, 7.5 m, and 1.75 m) do not match the computed value from the provided formula and parameters.

questions.explanation: Angular velocity is the time rate at which an object rotates or revolves about an axis. Angular velocity is represented by the Greek letter omega (ω, sometimes Ω). It is measured in angle per unit time; hence, the SI unit of angular velocity is radians per second. Also;Angular velocity is the vector measure of the rotation rate, which refers to how fast an object rotates or revolves relative to another point. In simple words, angular velocity is the time rate at which an object rotates or revolves about an axis.

questions.text: A particle is moving in a circle of radius 'r'. In one period of revolution, its displacement and distance covered are:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option suggests that the particle's displacement is 2πr and the distance covered is 2πr during one period of revolution. Displacement is a vector quantity that measures the shortest distance from the initial to the final position of the particle, and the distance covered measures the total path length. Therefore, option A is incorrect.

This option suggests that the particle's displacement is π and the distance covered is 2πr during one period of revolution. Displacement is a vector quantity, and its magnitude represents the shortest path from the initial to the final position of the particle. The distance covered represents the total path length. Therefore, option B is incorrect.

This option suggests that the particle's displacement is 2π and the distance covered is 2πr during one period of revolution. This option implies that the particle completes one full revolution during one period of revolution, which is not accurate. Therefore, option C Therefore, option C is incorrect.

This option suggests that the particle's displacement is zero, and the distance covered is 2πr during one period of revolution. This is the correct answer. During one period of revolution, the particle starts and ends at the same position (initial position). As a result, its displacement is zero. However, the particle covers the entire circumference of the circle during one revolution, which is 2πr (the distance covered). Therefore, option D is correct.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is option D: Zero, 2πr. The particle's displacement is zero, and the distance covered is 2πr during one period of revolution. During one period of revolution, the particle starts and ends at the same position (initial position). As a result, its displacement is zero. However, the particle covers the entire circumference of the circle during one revolution, which is 2πr (the distance covered).

questions.text: A body is rotating in a circle of radius r, keeping the period of rotation constant, but the radius is doubled(2r)then the centripetal force becomes:

This option incorrectly suggests that the centripetal force remains the same when the radius is doubled. According to the formula Fc=mrw2, the centripetal force changes when the radius is altered.

This option correctly states that the centripetal force becomes double when the radius is doubled. This is supported by the formula Fc=mrw2, which shows the relationship between force, mass, radius, and angular velocity.

This option incorrectly suggests that the centripetal force becomes four times when the radius is doubled. According to the formula Fc=mrw2, the force does not increase by a factor of four when the radius is doubled.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is option C: Double. When the radius of a rotating body is doubled, the centripetal force becomes double, as shown by the formula:Fc=mrw2Fc=m(2r)w2Fc=2mrw2This explains why option C is correct, while the other options are incorrect due to misinterpretation of the relationship between radius, mass, and angular velocity in the centripetal force formula.

questions.text: The expression for centripetal force is given by:

questions.text: When a body is whirled in a horizontal circle by means of a string, the centripetal force is supplied by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: It can be used to measure the centripetal force, but it does not supply centripetal force.

Yes, the centripetal force is supplied by the tension in the string. When a body is whirled in a horizontal circle by means of a string, the string exerts a force on the body towards the centre of the circle. This force is called the centripetal force. The centripetal force is necessary to keep the body moving in a circular path. The tension in the string is the force that the string exerts on the body. The tension in the string is equal to the centripetal force. The tension in the string is given by the following equation:T = Fc = mv2/r

Velocity is related to the kinetic energy and momentum of the body and is a component in the centripetal force formula, but it does not supply the force. Velocity's direction is constantly changing due to the centripetal force.
Centripetal acceleration is the result of the centripetal force acting on the mass, not the source of the force.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct. Yes, the centripetal force is supplied by the tension in the string. When a body is whirled in a horizontal circle by means of a string, the string exerts a force on the body towards the centre of the circle. This force is called the centripetal force. The centripetal force is necessary to keep the body moving in a circular path. The tension in the string is the force that the string exerts on the body. The tension in the string is equal to the centripetal force. The tension in the string is given by the following equation:T = Fc = mv2/r

questions.text: A body of mass 10kg, is moving with a velocity of 5m/s in a circle of radius 5m, what is the centripetal acceleration of the body?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: To find the centripetal acceleration of the body moving in a circle, we can use the formula:Centripetal acceleration (ac) = (velocity squared) / radiusGiven:Mass of the body (m) = 10 kgVelocity of the body (v) = 5 m/sRadius of the circle (r) = 5 mFirst, let's calculate the centripetal acceleration using the provided values:Centripetal acceleration (ac) = (5 m/s)2 / 5 mac = 25 m²/s² / 5 mac = 5 m/s²The centripetal acceleration of the body is 5 m/s².

questions.text: The ratio of angular speed of the minute hand of clock to that of its hour hand is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect according to the calculations.
This option is incorrect according to the calculations.
This option is incorrect according to the calculations.

The minute hand of a clock completes one revolution (360 degrees) in one hour or 60 minutes. Therefore, its angular speed isωminute = (360 degrees) / (60 minutes) = 6 degrees per minuteThe hour hand of a clock completes one revolution in 12 hours or 720 minutes. Therefore, its angular speed is:ωhour = (360 degrees) / (720 minutes) = 0.5 degrees per minuteThe ratio of the angular speed of the minute hand to that of the hour hand is:ωminute / ωhour = (6 degrees per minute) / (0.5 degrees per minute) = 12Therefore, the ratio of the angular speed of the minute hand to that of the hour hand is 12:1.

questions.explanation: The clock displays 12 divisions, which are 1 hour each for the hour hand and 5 minutes each for the minute hand. The minute hand completes 1 whole rotation of the 12 divisions for the hour hand to move 1 whole division; hence, the minute hand has to be 12 times faster than the hour hand.The minute hand of a clock completes one revolution (360 degrees) in one hour or 60 minutes. Therefore, its angular speed isωminute = (360 degrees) / (60 minutes) = 6 degrees per minuteThe hour hand of a clock completes one revolution in 12 hours or 720 minutes. Therefore, its angular speed is:ωhour = (360 degrees) / (720 minutes) = 0.5 degrees per minuteThe ratio of the angular speed of the minute hand to that of the hour hand is:ωminute / ωhour = (6 degrees per minute) / (0.5 degrees per minute) = 12Therefore, the ratio of the angular speed of the minute hand to that of the hour hand is 12:1.

questions.text: The direction of centripetal force is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Yes, the direction of the centripetal force is towards the centre of the circle. The centripetal force is the force that keeps an object moving in a circular path. The force is always directed towards the centre of the circle, regardless of the direction of the object's motion. The centripetal force is given by the following equation:Fc = mv²/r where:Fc is the centripetal force (N).m is the mass of the object (kg).v is the speed of the object (m/s).r is the radius of the circle (m).The centripetal force is always perpendicular to the object's velocity. This means that the centripetal force does not do any work on the object. The work done by a force is given by the following equation: W = F * d.

The direction of the centripetal force is perpendicular to the tangential velocity. The centripetal force is the force that keeps an object moving in a circular path. It is always directed towards the centre of the circle. The tangential velocity is the velocity of the object that is tangent to the circle. It is always directed along the tangent of the circle.
No, the direction of the centripetal force is not away from the centre of the circular path. The centripetal force is the force that keeps an object moving in a circular path.

No, the direction of centripetal force is perpendicular to the axis of rotation. The centripetal force is the force that keeps an object moving in a circular path. The force is always directed towards the centre of the circle. The centripetal force can be calculated from the following equation:Fc = mv²/r

questions.explanation: Option A is correct. The direction of the centripetal force is towards the centre of the circle. The centripetal force is the force that keeps an object moving in a circular path. The force is always directed towards the centre of the circle, regardless of the direction of the object's motion. The centripetal force is given by the following equation:Fc = mv²/r where:Fc is the centripetal force (N).m is the mass of the object (kg).v is the speed of the object (m/s).r is the radius of the circle (m).The centripetal force is always perpendicular to the object's velocity. This means that the centripetal force does not do any work on the object. The work done by a force is given by the following equation: W = F * d.

questions.text: A body is moving in a circular path with constant speed. The magnitude of tangential and centripetal acceleration are:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect according to the explanation.

Since the body is moving in a circular path with constant speed, the magnitude of:Tangential acceleration (at) is ZERO, because the speed is constant and there is no change in velocity along the tangent.Centripetal acceleration (ac) is NON-ZERO, because the direction of velocity is constantly changing due to the circular motion.The magnitude of centripetal acceleration (ac) can be calculated using the formula:ac = v² / rwhere v is the constant speed and r is the radius of the circular path.So, to summarise:at = 0 (tangential acceleration is zero)ac ≠ 0 (centripetal acceleration is non-zero)
This option is incorrect according to the explanation.
This option is incorrect according to the explanation.

questions.explanation: When a body moves in a circular path with constant speed, the tangential acceleration is zero because the speed remains constant. On the other hand, the centripetal acceleration is non-zero as it is responsible for changing the direction of velocity in circular motion. The correct answer is Option B because it correctly explains the relationship between tangential and centripetal acceleration in this scenario. Options A, C, and D are incorrect as they do not provide an accurate explanation based on the given conditions.For constant speed, Δv = 0, at = Δv/Δt = 0 ⇒ ac = v2/r.

questions.text: For positive angular displacement, the rotation would be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Clockwise rotation is conventionally associated with negative angular displacement, not positive.

For positive angular displacement, the rotation would be counterclockwise.The convention for determining the direction of rotation in angular displacement is typically based on the right-hand rule. If you align your right-hand fingers along the axis of rotation in a way that your thumb points in the positive direction of the axis, then the direction your fingers curl is considered to be the positive direction of rotation.
"Parallel" describes a spatial relationship, not a direction of rotation, and does not relate to angular displacement in this context.

Like "parallel", "perpendicular" is a spatial relationship. The angular displacement vector itself is perpendicular to the plane of rotation (along the axis of rotation), but the rotation is not described as "perpendicular" to anything in this way.

questions.explanation: For positive angular displacement, the rotation would be counterclockwise. The convention for determining the direction of rotation in angular displacement is typically based on the right-hand rule. If you align your right-hand fingers along the axis of rotation in a way that your thumb points in the positive direction of the axis, then the direction your fingers curl is considered to be the positive direction of rotation.

questions.text: The ratio of angular frequency and linear frequency is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. The angular frequency (ω) is 2π times the linear frequency (f), based on the relationship ω = 2πf.
This option is incorrect. While π is a common factor in circular motion, the correct ratio between angular and linear frequency is 2π, not π.
This is incorrect. The conversion factor between angular and linear frequency is not 1/2π, but rather 2π.
This option is not correct. The ratio of angular to linear frequency is not π/2, but 2π.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 2π. The relationship between angular frequency (ω) and linear frequency (f) is given by the formula ω = 2πf. This means that for each complete cycle of linear frequency, the angular displacement is 2π radians. Therefore, the ratio of angular frequency to linear frequency is 2π. The other options are incorrect because they do not correctly represent this fundamental relationship: π and π/2 are common misinterpretations, and 1/2π is the reciprocal of the correct ratio.

questions.text: Under what condition an object will have zero displacement but non-zero distance?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In linear motion, an object moves along a straight line. If an object has zero displacement, it means that it ends up at the same position as it started. In this case, the distance travelled by the object will also be zero, as there is no change in position. Therefore, linear motion does not fulfil the condition of having zero displacement but a non-zero distance.

The correct answer is Circulatory motion. Circulatory motion results in zero displacement but non-zero distance because the object returns to its initial position after completing a circular path. Oscillation, on the other hand, does not result in zero displacement because: Oscillation involves back-and-forth motion between two fixed points (e.g., a pendulum or a spring). The object does not return to its initial position after one cycle; instead, it oscillates between two points. Displacement occurs during each cycle, even if the object returns to its starting point after multiple cycles. In oscillation, the distance travelled is non-zero, and the displacement is also non-zero (although it may be periodic). In contrast, circulatory motion involves a complete cycle around a closed path, resulting in zero displacement. So, while both circulatory motion and oscillation involve repeated motion, the key difference lies in the path and the resulting displacement.

The random motion refers to the unpredictable and irregular movement of objects. In this type of motion, an object's displacement and distance can vary widely. While an object can have zero displacement and non-zero distance during random motion, it is not a necessary condition. The object could have any combination of displacement and distance, including both zero and non-zero values.

Oscillation refers to the back-and-forth motion around a central equilibrium point. In oscillatory motion, an object typically has zero displacement when it returns to its equilibrium position. However, the distance travelled by the object will depend on the amplitude of the oscillation. If the amplitude is zero, the object remains at the equilibrium position, resulting in both zero displacement and distance. If the amplitude is non-zero, the object will have a non-zero displacement and distance.

questions.explanation: In circular motion, an object moves along a circular path. If an object completes one full revolution and returns to its starting point, it will have zero displacement since the net change in position is zero. However, the object will have travelled a non-zero distance along the circular path. The distance travelled is equal to the circumference of the circle. Therefore, circular motion satisfies the condition of having zero displacement but a non-zero distance.

As the given sentence refers to each member individually, it will take a singular helping verb, i.e, has with it. Therefore, option A is accurate.

questions.text: Use an appropriate article:One of the students said, "_ professor is late today."

options.explanation: The indefinite article (a) is used before a noun that is general or when its identity is not known. "A" is used before words that start with a consonant sound.
The indefinite article "an" is used before a general noun or when its identity is unknown, specifically before words that start with a vowel sound.
The definite article "the" is employed preceding a noun to signify that the identity of the noun is recognized by the reader.

questions.text: I advised her _ drink it.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: After verbs like advise, when followed by a person (her), we need "to + verb" (an infinitive)."Don't" is an imperative or a direct command, not the right structure after "advise".

The correct answer is: "not to"I advised her not to drink it.Explanation: After verbs like "advice", when followed by a person, we use "to + verb", and to make it negative, we use "not to + verb".So:advise + person + (not) to + verbExample: I advised him to go. / I advised him not to go.
"to not" + verb can be used, especially in casual speech, "not to" is preferred and more natural in standard English.
After "to", we need the base form of the verb (without "don't")."Don't" is already a negative auxiliary verb — combining it with "to" is grammatically incorrect.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: "not to"I advised her not to drink it.Explanation: After verbs like "advise", when followed by a person, we use "to + verb", and to make it negative, we use "not to + verb".So:advise + person + (not) to + verbExample: I advised him to go. / I advised him not to go.

questions.text: What should come next to infirmary?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Surgery: Incorrect because a surgery typically takes place in an operating room, not directly after an infirmary.
Disease: Incorrect because a disease is a medical condition and not a physical location like the following word should be.

An infirmary is a place that takes care of the infirm, sick, or injured. Without patients, there is no infirmary. Surgery (choice a) may not be required for patients. A disease (choice b) is not necessary because the infirmary may only see patients with injuries. A receptionist (choice d) would be helpful but not essential. So the only option left is (choice c) C, so it is correct.
Receptionist: Incorrect because a receptionist is a job role and not a sequential step after an infirmary which requires a different facility or action.

questions.explanation: An infirmary is a place that takes care of the infirm, sick, or injured.Without patients, there is no infirmary. Surgery (choice a) may not be required for patients. A disease (choice b) is not necessary because the infirmary may only see patients with injuries. A receptionist (choice d) would be helpful but not essential. So the only option left is (choice c) C so it is correct.

questions.text: Statement:I. Majority of the citizens in the locality belongs to higher income group.II. The sales in the local supermarket are comparatively much higher than in other localities.

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
options.explanation: The reason why sales are comparatively high in a particular locality is because the residents of the locality are capable of paying high prices. So option A is correct.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: Option B is incorrect because there is no indication that statement II (higher sales in the local supermarket) causes statement I (majority of citizens in the locality belonging to a higher income group).

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: Option C is incorrect because there is no evidence that both statements are independent causes. They may or may not have any causal relationship with each other.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: Option D is incorrect because there is no information to suggest that both statements are effects of independent causes. Their relationship, if any, is not specified in the given information.

questions.explanation: The reason why sales are comparatively high in a particular locality is because the residents of the locality are capable of paying high prices. So option A is correct.

questions.text: Statement:The availability of imported fruits has increased in the indigenous market and so the demand for indigenous fruits has been decreased.Course of Action:I. To help the indigenous producers of fruits, the Government should impose high import duty on these fruits, even if these are not of good quality.II. The fruit vendors should stop selling imported fruits. So that the demand for indigenous fruits, would be increased.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect because both course of actions are not valid. Imposing high import duty on low-quality fruits may not necessarily help indigenous producers, and it could lead to adverse effects on consumers.

The ideas suggested in both I and II represent unfair means to cut competition. The correct way would be to devise methods and techniques such that the indigenous producers could produce better quality fruits and make them available in the market at prices comparable with those of the imported ones. Hence, neither I nor II follows.
Option C is incorrect because only course of action I is not valid. Imposing high import duty on low-quality fruits without considering the quality aspect can have negative consequences.

Option D is incorrect because only course of action II is not valid. Stopping the sale of imported fruits might not necessarily increase the demand for indigenous fruits, as consumer preferences and choices vary.

questions.explanation: The ideas suggested in both I and II represent unfair means to cut competition. The correct way would be to devise methods and techniques such that the indigenous producers could produce better quality fruits and make them available in the market at prices comparable with those of the imported ones. Hence, neither I nor II follows.
